rationale: LeBoeuf (2006) is not only listed in a literature cluster; it is explicitly used in a dedicated discussion of the date/delay effect. The citing paper summarizes that effect and then argues that its own subjective-time account can explain it, linking the prior finding to the paper's broader theoretical claims. The authors also report an additional test comparing delay versus date descriptions of duration, which gives the cited study a meaningful interpretive role.
relation_description: The citing paper uses LeBoeuf (2006) as key prior evidence for the date/delay discounting effect and then provides a mechanism-level reinterpretation based on subjective time perception. In this sense, it refines how that earlier effect is explained rather than merely mentioning it as background.
